title: Add backchannel push for browser tab using BroadcastChannel + Socket fanout
business_context: User opens 3 tabs of app. Server sends one notification per user; all tabs show it. Need server to dedupe per user (not per socket) and each tab to coordinate via BroadcastChannel.
constraints: Active tab shows toast; background tab updates badge only
Disconnect on one tab doesn't drop user

before_state.description: Every tab shows its own toast.

reasoning: Server broadcasts to user:userId room - each tab connection joins same room. Server emits once; all 3 sockets receive. Client picks 'leader tab' via BroadcastChannel + leader election; only leader displays toast, others increment badge. If leader closes, another becomes leader.

after_state.description: Per-user room + tab leader election; one toast across tabs.

files.purpose: Leader shows toast; non-leaders update badge

migration_strategy: Step 1: Verify server uses per-user room
testing_strategy: Use a fixture for Add backchannel push for browser tab using BroadcastChannel + Socket fanout that exercises the owned boundary, then add: happy-path test, unauthorized/invalid-input test, retry or concurrency simulation, persistence/contract assertion, and rollback/regression test. Assert the observable output, database/cache side effect, emitted event or log, and one failure metric. Run the suite in CI with deterministic seeds and a production-shaped fixture so fs-151 cannot pass through a mocked happy path only.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A naive solution for Add backchannel push for browser tab using BroadcastChannel + Socket fanout handles the demo path but misses the realtime e2e boundary: authorization, persistence, reconnect or retry behavior, rollback, and authorization denial count are not proved under production-shaped fixtures.

title: Add live cursor-position broadcast with bounded buffer per room
business_context: Live drawing app sends cursor positions at 60Hz. Server forwards all - heavy. Buffer per room, send batched at 30Hz.
constraints: Send latest per user per batch

files.content:
import { Server } from 'socket.io';
export function attachCursorNamespace(io: Server) { const ns = io.of('/cursors'); ns.on('connection', s => s.on('move', (room, pos) => s.to(room).emit('move', { userId: 'x', pos }))); }

reasoning: Server-side buffer { room: { userId: latestPos } }. Flush every 33ms; emit array per room. Drops intermediate positions automatically. For fs-158, this matters in Add live cursor-position broadcast with bounded buffer per room: the owner boundary must be explicit, the negative fixture must fail before the fix, and idempotency replay count must be observable during rollout.

title: Add Subresource Integrity (SRI) for CDN-hosted scripts
business_context: App loads vendor scripts from CDN. Compromise of CDN injects malicious code. Need SRI hashes on all external scripts.

files.content: <script src="https://cdn.example.com/vendor.js"></script>

reasoning: Build step computes SHA384 of vendor.js. HTML template uses computed hash. CSP also restricts script-src. If file changes, hash mismatches and browser refuses.

after_state.description: SRI hashes embedded in HTML build output.

files.content:
<!doctype html>
<html>
<body>
<script src="https://cdn.example.com/vendor.js" integrity="__SRI_VENDOR__" crossorigin="anonymous"></script>
</body>
</html>
files.purpose: Template with placeholder replaced during build
